messages.content: Hello! How may I assist you today? Please provide a specific question or topic related to DEW21 Energy's terms and conditions or data protection policies.

I am a DEW21 service agent. I have a customer case where a billing error was discovered today, but the error actually started 5 years ago. According to Section 16.2 of the Electricity Terms, how many years of overcharged payments are we legally obligated to refund or offset for this customer?

According to Section 16.2 of the Allgemeine Lieferbedingungen Strom (General Delivery Conditions for Electricity), the claim for refunds due to billing errors is limited to a maximum of three years, unless the impact of the error can be determined over a longer period.

The relevant clause explicitly states:

- "Claims under this clause are limited to the billing period preceding the detection of the error, unless the impact of the error can be determined over a longer period; in this case, the claim is limited to a maximum of three years."

Given that the billing error was discovered today and it started 5 years ago, the legal obligation for DEW21 Energy to refund or offset payments is limited to the most recent three years.

Sources: 
- [Allgemeine_Lieferbedingungen_Strom.pdf_en.txt] Section 16.2

I am a DEW21 service agent. I have a customer case where a billing error was discovered today, but the error actually started 5 years ago. According to Section 16.2 of the Electricity Terms, how many years of overcharged payments are we legally obligated to refund or offset for this customer?

According to Section 16.2 of the Allgemeine Lieferbedingungen Strom (General Delivery Conditions for Electricity), DEW21 is legally obligated to refund or adjust overcharged amounts within a maximum of three years from when the error was detected.

So, even though the billing error started 5 years ago, you are only required to address and refund any overcharges found in the last three years since the error was discovered today.
